purpose: Identifies the defining yield curve dynamic of the coming decade: the collision between pension duration demand and fiscal supply expansion
components: $55-60T in global pension assets (~60% DB) creating mandate-driven long-duration demand
Aging-driven fiscal deterioration expanding Treasury/sovereign bond supply
10Y-30Y segment as the fulcrum where opposing forces meet
Pension bid as informational degradation of BBB spreads (buyers purchasing for duration, not risk compensation)
UK LDI crisis as N=1 for fragility under stress
current_assessment: The most actionable rates framework from the synthesis. Forces reinforced each other 2000-2020 but have now diverged. The 10s30s steepener thesis is directionally supported at 6/10 but specific magnitude (20-50bp) is unfounded.
limitations: UK LDI crisis is N=1, limiting generalizability of stress dynamics
Shift from DB to DC pensions reduces the duration demand component over time
Central bank intervention (QE) can temporarily overwhelm both forces
Specific magnitude of curve steepening is not reliably estimable
